title: All branches in a conditional structure should avoid having exactly the same implementation
description: All branches in a conditional structure should not have exactly the same implementationAnalyze your codeintentionality-clearreliabilityBugWhy is this an issue?Having all branches of acaseorifchain with the same implementation indicates a problem.In the following code:if b == 0 # Noncompliant doOneMoreThing() else doOneMoreThing() end b = a > 12 ? 4 : 4; # Noncompliant case i # Noncompliant when 1 doSomething() when 2 doSomething() when 3 doSomething() else doSomething() endEither there is a copy-paste error that needs fixing or an unnecessarycaseorifchain that needs removing.ExceptionsThis rule does not apply toifchains norcasewithoutelse.if b == 0 # no issue, this could have been done on purpose to make the code more readable doSomething() elsif b == 1 doSomething() endAvailable In:Catch
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

noncompliant:
# Noncompliant: All branches have the same implementation
if b == 0
  doOneMoreThing()
else
  doOneMoreThing()
end

# Noncompliant: Case with identical implementations
case status
when 'active'
  send_notification()
when 'inactive'
  send_notification()
when 'pending'
  send_notification()
else
  send_notification()
end
compliant:
# Compliant: Different implementations
if user.admin?
  render_admin_dashboard
else
  render_user_dashboard
end

# Compliant: Case with different implementations
case status
when 'active'
  enable_user_account
when 'inactive'
  disable_user_account
when 'pending'
  send_verification_email
else
  log_unknown_status
end

checks.message: All branches in conditional structure have the same implementation
